# Chat-creation module boundary inside apps/agentos: the T0 decision record

## Context

T0.1 gate of the #13349 horizon map (pillar-2 keeper flow). The operator's #14577 veto binds the topology: **ONE harness app — `apps/agentos` — with the fleet manager as a module inside it.** The chat-creation surface is the SECOND module in that same app, and every T1–T7 view leaf mounts into the namespace this record names.

## The Problem

`apps/agentos` currently has a flat `view/` (Viewport, FleetSettingsPanel, DockPreview, Accounts) with no module seams. Two modules are now mandated to coexist (FM + chat-creation) with more implied (settings, the v14 home). Without a recorded module boundary, the second module's leaves repeat the accretion the operator called "uber brittle."

## The Fix

A short decision record (ticket-resolution comment, per the house pattern) naming: (1) the module directory convention inside `apps/agentos` (e.g. `view/fleet/`, `view/create/` — align with whatever the FM module correction lands, #14577's execution); (2) the chat-creation module's namespace + mount point in the Viewport (dock-participating region, consuming the #13158 contract); (3) the module-boundary contract (modules share the token floor + stores by explicit import, never by reach-around); (4) the shell-agnostic mount clause (dev-server now, Electron hosts later — the app never knows the shell).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Decision recorded with the FM-module correction cross-referenced (one convention, both modules).
- [ ] Namespace named; all #13349 T1–T7 leaves can cite it.
- [ ] Module-boundary contract stated (token floor + explicit store imports).
- [ ] Shell-agnostic mount clause stated.

## Out of Scope

Any view implementation; the FM module's own correction (Vega's veto execution); the design SSOT (T0.3 sibling).

## Related

Parent: #13349 (T0.1). #14577 (the veto authority) · #13158 · #13012.

## Timeline

### @neo-fable - 2026-07-04T03:38:53Z

## DECISION RECORD — the apps/agentos module convention (T0.1, 2026-07-04)

**Authority:** the #14577 operator veto (*one harness app; FM as a module inside it*) + this leaf's mandate to make the convention explicit for BOTH modules before any second-module view lands.

### 1. Module directory convention

`apps/agentos/view/<module>/` — one directory per product module, the flat legacy views absorbed as modules land:

- `view/fleet/` — the FM cockpit module (Vega's veto-execution relocates here; her call on internal structure).
- `view/create/` — the chat-creation module (this epic's T1–T3 surfaces).
- `view/` root keeps ONLY the app chrome: `Viewport.mjs` + `ViewportController.mjs` (the module composer) and shared primitives explicitly promoted to `view/shared/` when a second module consumes them.
- Legacy flat views (`FleetSettingsPanel`, `DockPreview`, `Accounts`) migrate into their owning module's directory as that module's tranches touch them — incremental demolition, never a big-bang move.

### 2. Mount contract

The Viewport composes modules as **dock-participating regions** (the #13158 container contract): each module exports one root view class; the Viewport mounts module roots into the dock layout; modules NEVER mount into each other. The chat-creation module's created-widget panes join the SAME dock plane as module-root siblings (a created app is a peer surface, not a child of the chat pane — the co-habitation topology from the harness thesis).

### 3. Module-boundary contract

- **Token floor shared:** all modules consume the app-level tokens (`apps/agentos/resources/` post-relocation) — no module-local color/type/spacing constants.
- **Stores per module, imports explicit:** a module owning a store exports it from its module directory; cross-module consumption is an explicit import cited in the consumer's JSDoc — never reach-around via globals or the Viewport.
- **Controllers stay module-scoped;** app-level routing/orchestration lives in `ViewportController` only.

### 4. Shell-agnostic mount clause

The app boots dev-server standalone exactly as today (`apps/agentos/index.html`); the Electron shell (#13033) HOSTS the app when Lane A lands — no module may import shell APIs; shell-conditional behavior enters via config, not detection.

**Consumers:** every #13349 T1–T7 leaf cites `view/create/`; #14560's tranche leaves cite `view/fleet/` (Vega's execution aligns — flagged for her concurrence as she's mid-relocation; the operator veto is the standing authority either way). **Closing condition:** Vega's one-line concurrence that her corrective PR's paths match §1 — then this leaf closes as delivered-by-record.
